subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Explain the biochemical steps of photosynthesis and the role of stomata
Compare aerobic and anaerobic respiration pathways in different organisms
Describe the human digestive system and the role of specific enzymes
Explain the double circulation system and the structure of the human heart
Understand the mechanism of urine formation in the nephron
Compare transportation mechanisms in plants (ascent of sap vs. translocation)

lesson_plan.title: Nutrition in Plants & Stomatal Mechanism
teaching_strategy: Discussion on the three main steps of photosynthesis
Microscopic view/Slides of stomata
Flowchart of energy conversion
student_activities: Draw the cross-section of a leaf labeling chloroplasts
Predict what happens if stomata are blocked with vaseline
assessment: Short answer: Role of guard cells

lesson_plan.title: Human Digestive System & Enzymes
topics_covered: Role of Gastric glands (HCl, Pepsin, Mucus)
Functions of Liver and Pancreas (Bile, Trypsin, Lipase)
teaching_strategy: Step-by-step digestion flowchart
Analogy: Enzymes as 'biological scissors'
Discussion on the length of small intestine in herbivores vs. carnivores
student_activities: Create a table mapping organs to the enzymes they secrete
Diagrammatically represent the role of Villi in absorption

lesson_plan.title: Respiration: Pathways of Glucose Breakdown
topics_covered: Pathways in Yeast, Muscle cells, and Mitochondria
teaching_strategy: The 'Glucose Fate' flowchart on the board
Comparing energy yield in aerobic vs. anaerobic respiration
Discussion on lactic acid and muscle fatigue
student_activities: Draw the flowchart of glucose breakdown
Calculate the difference in ATP production between the two modes
assessment: Exit slip: Why is ATP called energy currency?

lesson_plan.title: Human Respiratory System
topics_covered: Alveoli as the site of gas exchange
teaching_strategy: Lung model demonstration (Bell jar experiment)
Explaining diffusion of gases in Alveoli
Discussion on residual volume and Hemoglobin
student_activities: Draft a paragraph explaining how pressure changes lead to inhalation
Observe a chart of the respiratory tree
assessment: Short written test on breathing mechanism

lesson_plan.title: Transportation: The Human Heart
teaching_strategy: 3D Heart model demonstration
Tracing the path of oxygenated vs. deoxygenated blood
Explaining why the left ventricular wall is thicker
student_activities: Draw a schematic diagram of double circulation
Measure pulse rate before and after 1 minute of jumping
assessment: Reasoning: Importance of separation of right/left sides

lesson_plan.title: Transportation in Plants & Excretion Intro
teaching_strategy: Discussion on how tall trees get water (Transpiration pull)
Comparison: Xylem (Physical) vs. Phloem (Biological/Active)
Chart of the urinary system
student_activities: List the components of the excretory system

lesson_plan.title: The Nephron & Revision
teaching_strategy: Diagrammatic walkthrough of Bowman’s capsule and Tubule
Discussion on selective reabsorption of glucose/salts
Chapter mind-map revision

teaching_aids: Slides of Leaf TS and Stomata
Bell jar and balloon lung model
teacher_tip: Students often confuse 'respiration' with 'breathing' and 'Xylem' with 'Phloem'. Use consistent color coding (Red for Oxygenated/Food, Blue for Deoxygenated/Water) throughout your charts and board work to provide a visual anchor.
